name: Coconut Almond Balls
recipeInstructions:
Tastes just like Almond Joy.
Line two large cookie sheets with waxed paper. Set a large wire cooling rack on the paper; set aside.
Place coconut in a bowl; set aside.
Pour corn syrup into a 1-cup glass measure. Microwave on high (100%) for 1 minute, or until it boils. Immediately pour over the coconut. Work the warm syrup into the coconut using the back of a wooden spoon until the coconut is thoroughly coated. This may take some time and there will be enough syrup.
Using about one level measuring tablespoon of coconut, shape into a ball by firmly pressing the coconut in your palm and then rolling it between both palms (HINT: Measure out all of the coconut first, then roll into balls). Place the balls 2 inches apart on the wire racks. Let them dry for 10 minutes.
Reroll the coconut balls so there are no loose ends sticking up.
Place milk chocolate and shortening in a 4-cup glass measure or 1 1/2 quart microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on high for 1 to 2 minutes, or until it can be stirred smoothly and is glossy; stir once or twice. Working quickly, spoon about one level measuring tablespoon of chocolate over each coconut ball, making sure the chocolate coats them and allowing excess to drip onto the waxed paper. While the chocolate coating is still soft, lightly press a whole almond on top of each ball. Let them stand to set, or place in the refrigerator. Store in a single layer in an airtight container. Keeps best if refrigerated. Makes 26.
From: Steve Herrick
Source: [1,001 Home Ideas, Feb 1990]
